questions.question: A rectangular prism has length 5 cm, width 3 cm, and height 4 cm. What is its volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of a rectangular prism = length × width × height = 5 × 3 × 4 = 60 cm³.

questions.question: A cylinder has radius 2 inches and height 7 inches. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
A sphere has radius 3 meters. Using π ≈ 3.14, approximately what is its volume?
A cone has radius 4 cm and height 9 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
A triangular prism has a triangular base with area 12 square inches and a height (depth) of 5 inches. What is the volume?
questions.explanation: Volume of any prism = base area × height = 12 × 5 = 60 in³.

questions.question: A cylinder has diameter 6 cm and height 10 cm. Using π ≈ 3.14, what is its volume to the nearest whole number?
questions.explanation: Radius = diameter ÷ 2 = 3 cm. Volume = πr²h = 3.14 × 3² × 10 = 3.14 × 9 × 10 = 282.6 ≈ 283 cm³.
